MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-20-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MARLON KITTLES' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. Defendants' assertion that the amendment is "unnecessary" and "does not add anything substantive to the complaint" is not a basis for denying leave to amend. Neither is potential "prejudice" to Defendants or Defendants' factual disagreement with the proposed allegations. If Defendants do not believe that the allegations support the claim for punitive damages, they may file an appropriate motion.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
